content.problemStatement:
**Pushing a Local Branch to GitHub**

You are working on a project stored in a GitHub repository named **`Github-Practice`**.
Your team asks you to develop a new feature called **`login-feature`**. This feature should be **created on a separate branch**, and the branch must be **pushed to GitHub so the team can review your work**.

**Your Tasks:** \
Write git commands for each step below : 

**Step 1**: Connect your local project to the remote GitHub repository (if not connected).

**Step 2**: Create a new local branch named login-feature.(Only create don't switch)

**Step 3**: Switch to this branch and prepare it for work.

**Step 4**: Push the login-feature branch to GitHub so it appears along with the other branches.

After completing this exercise, you should see `login-feature` listed inside the Branches section on GitHub.
